titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robin W. Winks Papers Relating to United States Intelligence Services and Officers
Winks (Robin W.) Papers Relating to United States Intelligence Services and Officers
titlestmt.author: by Lucretia Baskin

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 26, 2024 at 04:00

did.unittitle: Robin W. Winks papers relating to United States intelligence services and officers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, printed material, and videocassettes relating to United States intelligence services and officers, including the Office of Strategic Services (OSS), the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), and the Association of Former Intelligence Officers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 3 (videocassettes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Papers, 1955-1999. II. Videocassettes, 1984-2002, undated.

bioghist.head: Robin W. Winks (1930-2003)
bioghist.p: Robin William Evert Winks was born in Indiana on December 5, 1930. He graduated from the University of Colorado in 1952 and received an M.A. in Maori studies from Victoria University as a Fulbright Scholar in New Zealand. He received his Ph.D. from Johns Hopkins in 1957. Winks joined Yale's history department in 1957, serving as its chairman from 1996 to 1999. From 1999 to 2000, he was the Vyvyan Harmsworth visiting professor of American history at Oxford University. He was a prolific author and in 1987 published Cloak & Gown: Scholars in America's Secret War , an account of the Central Intelligence Agency's relationship with major American universities. Winks died in New Haven, Connecticut, on April 7, 2003.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robin W. Winks Papers Relating to United States Intelligence Services and Officers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, printed material, and videocassettes relating to United States intelligence services and officers, including the Office of Strategic Services (OSS), the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), and the Association of Former Intelligence Officers. Writings contain drafts of books, articles, and television show proposals by authors including Christopher M. Andrew, William E. Duff, Anthony Ross Potter, Russell Jack Smith, Bayard Stockton, Douglas L. Wheeler, and Robin W. Winks. Also included are newsletters, invitations, conference programs, and other papers from the Veterans Association of O.S.S., the Association of Former Intelligence Officers, and other organizations; booklets, articles, book reviews, and other papers relating to the CIA and OSS; and photographs used in the production of Winks's book Cloak & Gown . The collection also contains correspondence relating to Yale University Library's acquisition of articles from the CIA's journal Studies in Intelligence , along with photocopies of selected articles from the journal.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robin W. Winks Papers Relating to United States Intelligence Services and Officers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: United States. Office of Strategic Services

did.unittitle: Papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Duff, William. A Time for Spies , draft, printout (photocopy)
Correspondence relating to Studies in Intelligence
List of articles from Studies in Intelligence sent to Yale University Library
Photographs of statue of Nathan Hale at CIA headquarters (photocopies)
Photographs used in production of Robin Winks's Cloak & Gown
Photographs of Norman Holmes Pearson used in production of Robin Winks's Cloak & Gown
Smith, Russell Jack. The Unknown CIA , draft, typescript (photocopy)
Andrew, Christopher M. U.S. Presidents as Intelligence Consumers and Managers (photocopy)
Proposal for "OSS: The Boys in Blue," a television series by Anthony R. Potter and Robin W. Winks (photocopy)
Publisher's proposal for Who Was Bill Harvey? by Bayard Stockton, with printout of Winks's email response to Stockton
Feingold, Steven A. The Cambridge Spy Ring and Anglo-American Intelligence Relations
Drafts and copies of articles by Christopher M. Andrew
Wheeler, Douglas L. A Global Perspective on International Espionage
Veterans of OSS newsletters, invitations, and correspondence
Conference and reunion ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes programs, schedules, and other information for conferences relating to The Canadian Association for Security and Intelligence Studies, The British Association for American Studies, the Vermont Symposium on Intelligence and Foreign Policy, and OSS Detachment 101 reunion.

did.unittitle: Association of Former Intelligence Officers chapter update and biography of Derek Armitage Lee
Association of Former Intelligence Officers convention bulletins
Association of Former Intelligence Officers New England Chapter newsletters and announcements
CIA and Historical Intelligence Collection booklets and information
Photocopies of book reviews and article relating to OSS reunion
Booklets and article offprints relating to OSS, intelligence, and the CIA
Articles
Studies in Intelligence , photocopies of articles
Studies in Intelligence , photocopies of articles
Videocassettes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily consists of home recordings of television programs and movies.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: "Blowback 97/04/02 Discovery Channel"
"The Brotherhood 97/03/31" and "Betrayal 04/01 (Discovery)"
"An Englishman Abroad"
"Enigma (2002)"
"Enigma Feature Screening Cassette"
"Frontline: The Spy Hunter"
"NOVA: Decoding Nazi Secrets"
"SOE--Englandspiel--BBC--1984"
